content_markdown:
1

###  Application and Definitions

1.1

Unless otherwise stated, this Part applies to:

  1. (1) a _large non-directive insurer_ ; and
  2. (2) a _Swiss general insurer_.

*Effective: 07/03/2016*

1.2

In this Part, the following definitions shall apply:

_commencement date_

has the meaning given in [Large Non-Solvency II Firms – Senior Managers Regime – Transitional Provisions](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-senior-managers-regime-transitional-provisions "Large Non-Solvency II Firms – Senior Insurance Managers Regime – Transitional Provisions").

_current approved person approval_

means

     1. (1) an approval granted to a _person_ under section 59 of _FSMA_ (Approval for particular arrangements):
        1. (a) by the _PRA_ for the performance of a _PRA senior management function_ ; or
        2. (b) by the _FCA_ for the performance of an _FCA-designated senior management function_ or a _significant influence function_ ;
     2. (2) a deemed approval given by:
        1. (a) the _PRA_ following the submission of a _conversion notification_ ; or
        2. (b) the _FCA_ under any equivalent process contained in rules in the _FCA Handbook_ ; or
     3. (3) for the purposes of [2.5](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-senior-managers-regime-applications-and-notifications#099d53776dcf4cd7be6a0621fc9074ab "2.5")(2) and [2.6](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-senior-managers-regime-applications-and-notifications#99e91651f8434e40947a9c1214b24ab9 "2.6")(1), an approval granted to that _person_ under section 59 of _FSMA_ by either the _PRA_ or the _FCA_ to perform a _controlled function_ prior to the _commencement date_ but which ceased
        1. (a) on or before the _commencement date_ ; and
        2. (b) during the six-_month_ period specified in [2.5](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-senior-managers-regime-applications-and-notifications#099d53776dcf4cd7be6a0621fc9074ab "2.5")(2) and [2.6](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-senior-managers-regime-applications-and-notifications#99e91651f8434e40947a9c1214b24ab9 "2.6")(1), as the case may be.

_regulatory body_

means any authority, body or _person_ having, or who has had, responsibility for the supervision or regulation of any _regulated activities_ or other financial services, whether within the _United Kingdom_ or overseas.

_significant influence function_

has the meaning given in the _FCA Handbook_.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

2

###  Application to Perform a Senior Management Function

2.1

The _PRA_ directs that a _firm_ must make a _PRA senior management approval application_ in accordance with [2.2](/pra-rules/non-solvency-ii-firms-senior-managers-regime-applications-and-notifications#691c4341fa6144f1ad832831eaf8b3cd "2.2"), before the activities requiring approval commence.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

2.2

The _PRA_ directs that a _firm_ must use Form A (long form) for a _PRA senior management approval application_ unless:

  1. (1) the _firm_ must use Form E under [2.3](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-senior-managers-regime-applications-and-notifications#1d6d03cc73d0418d945efb1f8cb7a8ea "2.3"); or
  2. (2) the _firm_ must use Form A (shortened form) under [2.6](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-senior-managers-regime-applications-and-notifications#99e91651f8434e40947a9c1214b24ab9 "2.6").

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

2.3

The _PRA_ directs that, subject to [2.4](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-senior-managers-regime-applications-and-notifications#6e38460dcaf8474281ce3f25086ea1ee "2.4") and [2.5](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-senior-managers-regime-applications-and-notifications#099d53776dcf4cd7be6a0621fc9074ab "2.5"), a _firm_ must use Form E for a _PRA senior management approval application_ if it is being made in respect of a _person_ who has a _current approved person approval_ and is either:

  1. (1) ceasing to perform a _PRA senior management function_ and taking up a new _PRA senior management function_ for the same _firm_ or another member of its _group_ ; or
  2. (2) ceasing to perform an _FCA-designated senior management function_ or a _significant influence function_ and taking up a _PRA senior management function_ for the _firm_ or another member of its _group_.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

2.4

The _PRA_ directs that a _firm_ must not use Form E for a _PRA senior management approval application_ if:

  1. (A1) a notification has been or should be made to the _PRA_ or _FCA_ under any of the following:
     1. (a) section 63(2A) of _FSMA_ (Duty to notify regulator of grounds for withdrawal of approval);
     2. (b) section 64C of _FSMA_ (Requirement for authorised person to notify regulator of disciplinary action);
  2. (1) a notification has been made or should be made to the _PRA_ under any of the rules in [Notifications 11](/pra-rules/notifications#24ae7653b5b24fe292939e4406080222 "Notifications 11") or to the _FCA_ under any equivalent provisions of the _FCA Handbook_ ;
  3. (2) any of the circumstances in [4.2](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-senior-managers-regime-applications-and-notifications#2454ff0538f340d9a240af8393d62701 "4.2")(2)(a) to (d) apply in relation to:
     1. (a) any _controlled function_ that that _person_ is ceasing to perform; or
     2. (b) any _controlled function_ that that _person_ is continuing to perform in relation to that _firm_ or a _firm_ in the same _group_.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

2.5

The _PRA_ directs that a _firm_ must not use Form E if the _person_ to whom the _PRA senior management approval application_ relates:

  1. (1) has never before been approved:
     1. (a) by the _PRA_ to perform a _controlled function_ ; or
     2. (b) by the _FCA_ to perform an _FCA-designated senior management function_ or a _significant influence function_
  2. for any _firm_;
  3. or
  4. (2) has not been the subject of a _current approved person approval_ in relation to any _firm_ for more than six _months_ prior to the date of application.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

2.6

  1. (1) The _PRA_ directs that a _firm_ must use Form A (shortened form) for a _PRA senior management approval application_ if:
     1. (a) the _person_ to whom the application relates:
        1. (i) has at the time of application a _current approved person approval_ ; or
        2. (ii) has had a _current approved person approval_ within the previous six _months_ ; and
     2. (b) there have been no matters arising in relation to the fitness and propriety of the _person_ to whom the _PRA senior management approval application_ relates which mean that the information provided to the _FCA_ or _PRA_ regarding fitness and propriety in connection with a _current approved person approval_ may have changed since the application for the _current approved person approval_ was made.
  2. (2) The _PRA_ directs that a _firm_ must not use Form A (shortened form) if the circumstances set out in [2.3](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-senior-managers-regime-applications-and-notifications#1d6d03cc73d0418d945efb1f8cb7a8ea "2.3") apply.

*Effective: 11/05/2023*

2.7

The _PRA_ directs that the form submitted for a _PRA senior management approval application_ must be accompanied by a _statement of responsibilities form_ or, where a _statement of responsibilities form_ has already been provided under [Large Non-Solvency II Firms – Key Function Holder – Notifications 2.2](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-key-function-holder-notifications#ea2815329bb546148d7f9bf81c0d5b84 "Large Non-Solvency II Firms – Key Function Holder – Notifications 2.2"), an updated version of it.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

3

###  Withdrawal of a PRA Senior Management Approval Application

3.1

The _PRA_ directs that a _firm_ withdrawing an outstanding _PRA senior management approval application_ must do so using Form B.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

4

###  Ceasing to Perform a PRA Senior Management Function

4.1

A _firm_ must notify the _PRA_ no later than seven _business days_ after a _person_ permanently ceases to perform a _PRA senior management function_ , using:

  1. (1) Form E pursuant to the direction in [2.3](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-senior-managers-regime-applications-and-notifications#1d6d03cc73d0418d945efb1f8cb7a8ea "2.3") if a _person_ permanently ceases to perform a _PRA senior management function_ and the _firm_ is also making an application for the same _person_ to perform a new _PRA senior management function_ ; and
  2. (2) in all other cases, Form C.

*Effective: 11/05/2023*

4.2

  1. (1) A _firm_ must notify the _PRA_ as soon as practicable after it becomes aware, or has information which reasonably suggests, that it will submit a qualified Form C in respect of a _PRA senior management function holder_.
  2. (2) Form C is qualified if the information it contains:
     1. (a) relates to the fact that the _firm_ has dismissed or suspended the _PRA senior management function holder_ from its employment;
     2. (b) relates to the resignation by the _PRA senior management function holder_ while under investigation by the _firm_ , the _PRA_ or any other _regulatory body_ ;
     3. (c) includes a notification under any of the provisions set out in [2.4](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-senior-managers-regime-applications-and-notifications#6e38460dcaf8474281ce3f25086ea1ee "2.4")(A1) or (1); or
     4. (d) otherwise reasonably suggests that it may affect the _PRA’s_ assessment of the _PRA senior management function holder’s_ fitness and propriety.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

4A

###  Application to Vary a Conditional Approval

4A.1

The _PRA_ directs that a _firm_ making an application to the _PRA_ under section 63ZA of _FSMA_ (for the variation of a conditional approval) must do so by submitting

  1. (1) Form I; and
  2. (2) a _statement of responsibilities form_ for the _PRA approved person_ concerned.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

4A.2

The _PRA_ directs that a _firm_ withdrawing an application made under section 63ZA of _FSMA_ (Variation of senior manager’s approval at request of relevant authorised person) must do so using Form B.

*Effective: 11/05/2023*

5

###  Change in Details Relating to PRA Senior Management Function Holders

5.1

If a _PRA senior management function holder's_ title, name or national insurance number changes, the _firm_ for which the _person_ performs a _PRA senior management function_ must notify the _PRA_ of that change within seven _business days_ of the _firm_ becoming aware of the matter, using Form D.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

5.1A

If a _PRA senior management function holder_ has been temporarily absent for more than a 12 week period, the _firm_ for which the _person_ performs a _PRA senior management function_ must notify the _PRA_ of:

  1. (1) that absence within seven _business days_ of the end of the 12 week period; and
  2. (2) the _PRA senior management function holder’s_ return from absence within seven _business days_ of the date of their return,

in each case, using Form D.

*Effective: 02/06/2021*

5.2

If, in relation to a _firm_ which has submitted an application on either Form A (long or shortened form) or Form E, as required by [2.2](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-senior-managers-regime-applications-and-notifications#9f71e02dc76c41b4a1c60f35ae6d638c "2.2"), any of the details relating to _PRA senior management functions_ are to change, the _firm_ must notify the _PRA_ using Form D as soon as reasonably practicable after the _firm_ becomes aware of the proposed change.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

5.3

The _PRA_ directs that if a _PRA senior management function holder_ ceases to perform a _PRA senior management function_ for a _firm_ but continues to perform one or more _PRA senior management functions_ for the same _firm_ , the _firm_ must submit a revised _statement of responsibilities form_ for the remaining _PRA senior management function(s)_ , along with Form J.

*Effective: 11/05/2023*

6

###  Procedure for Making Applications and Notifications

6.1

The _PRA_ directs that, save as required by [6.2](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-senior-managers-regime-applications-and-notifications#3f949d9d612d486cacc4c9d09afa50e9 "6.2"), a _firm_ must make any applications, notifications or submissions required by this Part by submitting the form specified using the _ONA system_.

*Effective: 07/03/2016*

6.2

If, under any direction or rule in this Part:

  1. (1) a _firm_ is required to make an application, notification or submission online; and
  2. (2) the information technology systems used by the _PRA_ fail and online submission is unavailable for 24 hours or more,

until such time as facilities for online submission are restored, a _firm_ must submit the specified form in the way set out in [Notifications 7](/pra-rules/notifications#8e186d20f3cf4d218117e0780f104f7c "Notifications 7").

*Effective: 07/03/2016*

6.3

  1. (1) The _PRA_ directs that, if a firm is required to submit a revised _statement of responsibilities_ under section 62A of _FSMA_ , it must do so by submitting Form J with the revised _statement of responsibilities form_.
  2. (2) The _PRA_ directs that a _firm _must not use Form J where the revisions are to be made as part of arrangements involving an application:
     1. (a) for approval for the _person_ performing the _PRA senior management function_ concerned to perform another _PRA senior management function_ or _relevant senior management function_ for the same _firm_ ; or
     2. (b) to vary under section 63ZA of _FSMA_ an approval for the _person_ performing the _PRA senior management function_ concerned to perform another _PRA senior management function_ or _relevant senior management function_ for the same _firm_.

*Effective: 11/05/2023*

7

###  Forms

7.1

  1. (1) Form A (long form) is available on the _PRA's_ website.
  2. (2) Form A (shortened form) is available on the _PRA's_ website.
  3. (3) Form B is available on the _PRA's_ website.
  4. (4) Form C may be found [here](https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/-/media/boe/files/prudential-regulation/authorisations/senior-managers-and-senior-insurance-managers-regimes-approvals/form-c-dec-2023.pdf "Form C").
  5. (5) Form D may be found [here](https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/-/media/boe/files/prudential-regulation/authorisations/senior-managers-and-senior-insurance-managers-regimes-approvals/form-d-dec-2023.pdf "Form D").
  6. (6) Form E is available on the _PRA's_ website.
  7. (6A) Form I is available on the _PRA's_ website.
  8. (6B) Form J is available on the _PRA's_ website.
  9. (7) The _statement of responsibilities form_ is available on the _PRA's_ website.

*Effective: 21/12/2023*
content_html:
1

Application and Definitions

1.1

Unless otherwise stated, this Part applies to:

  1. (1) a large non-directive insurer; and
  2. (2) a Swiss general insurer.
  • 07/03/2016

1.2

In this Part, the following definitions shall apply:

commencement date

has the meaning given in Large Non-Solvency II Firms – Senior Managers Regime – Transitional Provisions.

current approved person approval

means

    1. (1) an approval granted to a person under section 59 of FSMA (Approval for particular arrangements):
      1. (a) by the PRA for the performance of a PRA senior management function; or
      2. (b) by the FCA for the performance of an FCA-designated senior management function or a significant influence function;
    2. (2) a deemed approval given by:
      1. (a) the PRA following the submission of a conversion notification; or
      2. (b) the FCA under any equivalent process contained in rules in the FCA Handbook; or
    3. (3) for the purposes of 2.5(2) and 2.6(1), an approval granted to that person under section 59 of FSMA by either the PRA or the FCA to perform a controlled function prior to the commencement date but which ceased
      1. (a) on or before the commencement date; and
      2. (b) during the six-month period specified in 2.5(2) and 2.6(1), as the case may be.

regulatory body

means any authority, body or person having, or who has had, responsibility for the supervision or regulation of any regulated activities or other financial services, whether within the United Kingdom or overseas.

significant influence function

has the meaning given in the FCA Handbook.

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 1.2 before 10/12/2018

2

Application to Perform a Senior Management Function

2.1

The PRA directs that a firm must make a PRA senior management approval application in accordance with 2.2, before the activities requiring approval commence.

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 2.1 before 10/12/2018

2.2

The PRA directs that a firm must use Form A (long form) for a PRA senior management approval application unless:

  1. (1) the firm must use Form E under 2.3; or
  2. (2) the firm must use Form A (shortened form) under 2.6.
  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 2.2 before 10/12/2018

2.3

The PRA directs that, subject to 2.4 and 2.5, a firm must use Form E for a PRA senior management approval application if it is being made in respect of a person who has a current approved person approval and is either:

  1. (1) ceasing to perform a PRA senior management function and taking up a new PRA senior management function for the same firm or another member of its group; or
  2. (2) ceasing to perform an FCA-designated senior management function or a significant influence function and taking up a PRA senior management function for the firm or another member of its group.
  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 2.3 before 10/12/2018

2.4

The PRA directs that a firm must not use Form E for a PRA senior management approval application if:

  1. (A1) a notification has been or should be made to the PRA or FCA under any of the following:
    1. (a) section 63(2A) of FSMA (Duty to notify regulator of grounds for withdrawal of approval);
    2. (b) section 64C of FSMA (Requirement for authorised person to notify regulator of disciplinary action);
  2. (1) a notification has been made or should be made to the PRA under any of the rules in Notifications 11 or to the FCA under any equivalent provisions of the FCA Handbook;
  3. (2) any of the circumstances in 4.2(2)(a) to (d) apply in relation to:
    1. (a) any controlled function that that person is ceasing to perform; or
    2. (b) any controlled function that that person is continuing to perform in relation to that firm or a firm in the same group.
  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 2.4 before 10/12/2018

2.5

The PRA directs that a firm must not use Form E if the person to whom the PRA senior management approval application relates:

  1. (1) has never before been approved:
    1. (a) by the PRA to perform a controlled function; or
    2. (b) by the FCA to perform an FCA-designated senior management function or a significant influence function
  2. for any firm;
  3. or
  4. (2) has not been the subject of a current approved person approval in relation to any firm for more than six months prior to the date of application.
  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 2.5 before 10/12/2018

2.6

  1. (1) The PRA directs that a firm must use Form A (shortened form) for a PRA senior management approval application if:
    1. (a) the person to whom the application relates:
      1. (i) has at the time of application a current approved person approval; or
      2. (ii) has had a current approved person approval within the previous six months; and
    2. (b) there have been no matters arising in relation to the fitness and propriety of the person to whom the PRA senior management approval application relates which mean that the information provided to the FCA or PRA regarding fitness and propriety in connection with a current approved person approval may have changed since the application for the current approved person approval was made.
  2. (2) The PRA directs that a firm must not use Form A (shortened form) if the circumstances set out in 2.3 apply.
  • 11/05/2023
  • Past version of 2.6 before 11/05/2023

2.7

The PRA directs that the form submitted for a PRA senior management approval application must be accompanied by a statement of responsibilities form or, where a statement of responsibilities form has already been provided under Large Non-Solvency II Firms – Key Function Holder – Notifications 2.2, an updated version of it.

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 2.7 before 10/12/2018

3

Withdrawal of a PRA Senior Management Approval Application

3.1

The PRA directs that a firm withdrawing an outstanding PRA senior management approval application must do so using Form B.

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 3.1 before 10/12/2018

4

Ceasing to Perform a PRA Senior Management Function

4.1

A firm must notify the PRA no later than seven business days after a person permanently ceases to perform a PRA senior management function, using:

  1. (1) Form E pursuant to the direction in 2.3 if a person permanently ceases to perform a PRA senior management function and the firm is also making an application for the same person to perform a new PRA senior management function; and
  2. (2) in all other cases, Form C.
  • 11/05/2023
  • Past version of 4.1 before 11/05/2023

4.2

  1. (1) A firm must notify the PRA as soon as practicable after it becomes aware, or has information which reasonably suggests, that it will submit a qualified Form C in respect of a PRA senior management function holder.
  2. (2) Form C is qualified if the information it contains:
    1. (a) relates to the fact that the firm has dismissed or suspended the PRA senior management function holder from its employment;
    2. (b) relates to the resignation by the PRA senior management function holder while under investigation by the firm, the PRA or any other regulatory body;
    3. (c) includes a notification under any of the provisions set out in 2.4(A1) or (1); or
    4. (d) otherwise reasonably suggests that it may affect the PRA’s assessment of the PRA senior management function holder’s fitness and propriety.
  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 4.2 before 10/12/2018

4A

Application to Vary a Conditional Approval

4A.1

The PRA directs that a firm making an application to the PRA under section 63ZA of FSMA (for the variation of a conditional approval) must do so by submitting

  1. (1) Form I; and
  2. (2) a statement of responsibilities form for the PRA approved person concerned.
  • 10/12/2018

4A.2

The PRA directs that a firm withdrawing an application made under section 63ZA of FSMA (Variation of senior manager’s approval at request of relevant authorised person) must do so using Form B.

  • 11/05/2023
  • Past version of 4A.2 before 11/05/2023

5

Change in Details Relating to PRA Senior Management Function Holders

5.1

If a PRA senior management function holder's title, name or national insurance number changes, the firm for which the person performs a PRA senior management function must notify the PRA of that change within seven business days of the firm becoming aware of the matter, using Form D.

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 5.1 before 10/12/2018

5.1A

If a PRA senior management function holder has been temporarily absent for more than a 12 week period, the firm for which the person performs a PRA senior management function must notify the PRA of:

  1. (1) that absence within seven business days of the end of the 12 week period; and
  2. (2) the PRA senior management function holder’s return from absence within seven business days of the date of their return,

in each case, using Form D.

  • 02/06/2021

5.2

If, in relation to a firm which has submitted an application on either Form A (long or shortened form) or Form E, as required by 2.2, any of the details relating to PRA senior management functions are to change, the firm must notify the PRA using Form D as soon as reasonably practicable after the firm becomes aware of the proposed change.

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 5.2 before 10/12/2018

5.3

The PRA directs that if a PRA senior management function holder ceases to perform a PRA senior management function for a firm but continues to perform one or more PRA senior management functions for the same firm, the firm must submit a revised statement of responsibilities form for the remaining PRA senior management function(s), along with Form J.

  • 11/05/2023
  • Past version of 5.3 before 11/05/2023

6

Procedure for Making Applications and Notifications

6.1

The PRA directs that, save as required by 6.2, a firm must make any applications, notifications or submissions required by this Part by submitting the form specified using the ONA system.

  • 07/03/2016

6.2

If, under any direction or rule in this Part:

  1. (1) a firm is required to make an application, notification or submission online; and
  2. (2) the information technology systems used by the PRA fail and online submission is unavailable for 24 hours or more,

until such time as facilities for online submission are restored, a firm must submit the specified form in the way set out in Notifications 7.

  • 07/03/2016

6.3

  1. (1) The PRA directs that, if a firm is required to submit a revised statement of responsibilities under section 62A of FSMA, it must do so by submitting Form J with the revised statement of responsibilities form.
  2. (2) The PRA directs that a firm must not use Form J where the revisions are to be made as part of arrangements involving an application:
    1. (a) for approval for the person performing the PRA senior management function concerned to perform another PRA senior management function or relevant senior management function for the same firm; or
    2. (b) to vary under section 63ZA of FSMA an approval for the person performing the PRA senior management function concerned to perform another PRA senior management function or relevant senior management function for the same firm.
  • 11/05/2023
  • Past version of 6.3 before 11/05/2023

7

Forms

7.1

  1. (1) Form A (long form) is available on the PRA's website.
  2. (2) Form A (shortened form) is available on the PRA's website.
  3. (3) Form B is available on the PRA's website.
  4. (4) Form C may be found here.
  5. (5) Form D may be found here.
  6. (6) Form E is available on the PRA's website.
  7. (6A) Form I is available on the PRA's website.
  8. (6B) Form J is available on the PRA's website.
  9. (7) The statement of responsibilities form is available on the PRA's website.
  • 21/12/2023
  • Past version of 7.1 before 21/12/2023
